name: Taichung (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: the central mountain range and the city's lush greenery
the rivers and sky of central Taiwan

description: A green field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem combining curving forms in white and blue and the wordmark 'Taichung'.
history: Taichung, Taiwan's central metropolis and third-largest city, was merged with Taichung County on 25 December 2010 to form a special municipality. The current city flag was redesigned in 2025 with a refreshed wordmark and emblem; earlier flags dated from the 2010 merger and from the original Taichung City of 1947.

features.symbolism: the central mountains and lush greenery of central Taiwan
abstract curving forms representing the rivers and mountains of the Taichung basin, with the city wordmark 'Taichung' expressing its modern, dynamic identity
